argument: Cowork-Atlas should audit and fix all user-facing strings in Figma prototypes for compliance with LAW 3 (Shame-Free Language) and LAW 1 (Never Red). Current prototypes likely violate both: red error states, shame-based copy like 'You haven't started' or 'X% complete'. Only Cowork can access Figma MCP to inspect and修正 these. Task is independent, high-impact — prevents trauma-triggering UX at design phase. Output: updated Figma frames with compliant text and color, documented change log in Figma comments. Use Figma MCP to navigate pages, Supabase MCP to cross-check against current DB enums if needed.
strongest_counter: Code-Atlas could later override with non-compliant code, making this effort superficial.
evidence_cited: ECOSYSTEM-CONSTITUTION.md §LAW 1, LAW 3; voice.md examples prohibit design-code misalignment
